name: Plastic bottle
instructions: Empty and place in plastic packaging.
why: Plastic packaging should be sorted for recycling.

name: Plastic lid / cap
why: Plastic lids are plastic packaging.

name: Soft plastic / bag
why: Soft and hard plastic packaging are collected as plastic packaging.

name: Medicine blister pack (tray)
why: Medicine trays are included in plastic packaging examples.

name: Aluminium can
instructions: Empty and sort as metal packaging.
why: Cans are metal packaging.

name: Metal lid / bottle cap
why: Bottle caps and metal lids are metal packaging.

name: Metal tube
why: Metal tubes are listed as metal packaging.

name: Milk / juice carton
instructions: Empty, flatten, and sort as paper packaging.
why: Cartons from milk and similar are paper packaging.

name: Egg carton
why: Egg cartons are paper packaging.

name: Cardboard box / shoe box
instructions: Flatten and sort as paper packaging.
why: Corrugated cardboard and shoe boxes are paper packaging.

name: Paper cup
why: Paper cups are listed under paper packaging.

name: Newspaper / magazines
instructions: Keep dry and sort as newspapers/paper.
why: Newspapers and paper products are collected separately.

name: Writing / drawing paper
why: Writing and drawing paper is included.

name: Clear glass bottle / jar
why: Clear glass bottles and jars are sorted as clear glass.

name: Coloured glass bottle / jar
why: Coloured glass bottles and jars are sorted separately.

name: Drinking glass / vase / mirror (NOT packaging)
instructions: Do not put with glass packaging. Take to recycling centre if needed.
why: The guide says these should never be mixed with glass packaging.

name: Banana peel / fruit peel
why: Fruit and potato peel etc. are food waste.

name: Eggshells / coffee filters / tea bags
why: These are listed as food waste.

name: Diaper / pad / tampon
why: These are listed under residual waste.

name: Cigarette butt / snuff
why: These are listed under residual waste.

name: Toothbrush / cotton buds / razor
why: These are listed under residual waste.

name: Small electronics
instructions: Take to recycling centre or collection cabinet (Samlaren) when available.
why: The guide lists these as small electronics and mentions Samlaren for small devices.

name: Light bulb / LED / halogen
instructions: Take to recycling centre or Samlaren collection point.
why: Light bulbs and lamps are listed and can be deposited in Samlaren.

name: Small batteries
instructions: Take to Samlaren or hazardous waste truck / collection point.
why: Small batteries are listed; the guide mentions Samlaren and hazardous waste services.

name: Hazardous waste (paint/solvents/spray)
instructions: Take to recycling centre or hazardous waste truck.
why: These are listed as hazardous waste and the guide describes the hazardous waste truck.

name: Textiles (clean and dry)
why: Textiles are listed with the requirement clean and dry.

name: Bulky waste
instructions: Take to a staffed recycling centre.
why: Bulky waste is listed and the guide explains recycling centres handle this.

name: Garden waste
why: Garden waste is listed and recycling centres are mentioned for this.

name: Second hand items (reuse)
instructions: Bring to reuse/second-hand collection (e.g., Pop-up Returen when available).
why: Second-hand items and Pop-up Returen are mentioned for reuse/recycling.
